mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% triggers expectations that the Federal Reserve will pivot to monetary easing, which compresses equity discount rates and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% from a level below 4.0% within a 3-month window
regime_hint: late cycle when unemployment starts to increase
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of instances when the UNRATE crosses above 4.5% under these conditions over any 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production indicate real economic weakening and downward pressure on corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the mean 20-day forward return after INDPRO declines is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.1) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the CPI inflation index below a 0.2% change signals that inflationary pressures are cooling, which reduces the likelihood of further Fed tightening and boosts equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 55% of triggered events over the most recent 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflect a synchronized weakening in both labor demand and residential investment, which depresses aggregate demand and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and HOUST decline month-over-month in the same month
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of coincident decline months over the full available sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 5% and retail sales (RSAFS) increase at a strong pace (MoM > 0.5%), it indicates persistent demand that keeps monetary policy restrictive for longer, hurting equity valuations over a 60-trading-day horizon.
regime_hint: high rate regime with resilient consumer spending
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 40% of triggered quarters (using monthly signals) over any 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth at a robust pace above 3% signals a strong demand environment that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return following a GDPC1 release showing >3% annualized quarterly growth is not significantly positive (mean return <= 0, t-test p > 0.05) over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 150,000 workers signals labor market distress that precipitates fast-expected Fed easing, boosting equity prices over a short 5-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS drops by at least 150,000 over the full backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate of 25 basis points or more signals the commencement of an easing cycle, which reduces discount rates and supports higher equity valuations over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of events when FEDFUNDS decreases by 0.25 or more over any 3-year walk-forward period.
